behaviors.description: Renamed files are reported only in `renamed`, not double-counted as `added`+`deleted`.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real `git` rename signal handoff into the CLI (subprocess output + parser + categorizer). A fake diff fixture can miss real rename representations/threshold behavior, so mocking can hide this class of bug (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected lower level: behavior text is about repository-level rename reporting, not just a local pure function. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp real repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows 1 renamed, 0 added, 0 deleted for that change set.

behaviors.description: Binary diffs do not crash the tool; binary files are counted.
behaviors.justification: This specifically risks real subprocess/encoding behavior (`git diff` output for binary content, stdout/stderr decoding, exit handling). In-process mocks often return sanitized text and can mask decode/crash failures (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected lower level: behavior explicitly involves binary diffs from a real repo state, not only local parsing logic. Rejected higher levels: no model output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo with a real binary file (e.g., random bytes), commit baseline, modify binary, run CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary change is included in counts.

behaviors.description: Repo with no commits returns an empty report, not an error.
behaviors.justification: Critical failure comes from real `git` behavior in an unborn-HEAD repository (actual exit codes/messages), which is OS/subprocess dependent. Stubbed repo APIs can hide this exact edge case (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected lower level: behavior text is explicitly about a real repository state (`no commits yet`). Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML or multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh temp git repo with zero commits, run CLI, assert zero exit (or defined non-error status) and structured empty report on stdout.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s actual change history.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim over real git history semantics (commits, status transitions, possible rename/binary handling) and subprocess interaction. Mocking history responses can make mismatches invisible (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected lower level: behavior scope is repository history fidelity, not isolated algorithm-only logic. Rejected higher levels: deterministic non-LLM pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known scripted sequence (add/modify/delete/rename across commits), run CLI over target range, and assert each category count equals expected ground truth from the scripted history.

behaviors.description: Output is identical for equivalent staged vs unstaged changes.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics and how the CLI queries them via real commands. In-process fakes commonly flatten these states and can miss divergences (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly compares real staged/unstaged repository states. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependence and no agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change content twice: once staged and once unstaged (separate runs/reset as needed). Run CLI for each state and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
